note: Recommended end-game build per character — best-in-slot weapon, the three defining Pictos, the Luminas to run, where to put attribute points and how to play it. Attribute emphasis uses the in-game 0–99 scale; exact stat totals depend on your level and gear, so they're shown as priorities. Pictos and their mastered effects double as Luminas you can share across the party.

characters.name: Maelle
characters.summary: The party's damage engine. Stance-dance into Virtuose and unload near-100% crit hits without ever taking damage thanks to flawless parries. Glass Cannon + Immaculate turns her into a one-woman wipe button.

pictos.name: Glass Cannon
pictos.effect: 30%+ increased damage, but you can't be healed — pure offense.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Big damage bonus while you've taken no damage this battle (rewards perfect parrying).

pictos.name: Confident Fighter
pictos.effect: 30% increased damage but can't be Healed — stacks the all-in gameplan.

characters.statsFocus: Stack Critical Rate toward 100% and Speed for extra turns; Health/Defense come from the gear, not points.
playstyle: Open in Offensive stance, build to Virtuose, then dump your biggest skills during an extra turn.
Parry everything — Immaculate keeps your damage maxed only while you stay untouched.
Never rely on heals (Glass Cannon blocks them); survive with parries and Survivor.
pros: Highest single-target burst in the game
Snowballs once stances are set up

characters.name: Lune
characters.summary: Stains and elements. Lune banks elemental Stains to power up spells and loops Burn for big damage-over-time while supporting the party. Fast, flexible, and safe.

pictos.name: Empowering Parry
pictos.effect: A successful parry grants a damage buff — turns defense into offense.

pictos.name: Charging Critical
pictos.effect: Builds Critical Rate as you act, so your spells start landing crits.

pictos.name: Shortcut
pictos.effect: Reduces the AP cost of skills so you cast more per turn.

characters.statsFocus: Speed first (act often to keep Stains and Burn rolling), then Critical Rate.
playstyle: Generate elemental Stains early, then spend them on empowered spells.
Keep Burn applied for free damage-over-time while you set up.
Use her heals/cleanse when the party needs it — she's a flexible backbone.
cons: Stain management has a learning curve

characters.name: Verso
characters.summary: Lives on the edge. Verso climbs through combat Ranks (S being the peak) where his damage explodes, fuelled by parries and counters. Aggressive, precise, and devastating once he's at S-rank.

pictos.name: Perilous Parry
pictos.effect: Tighter parry window for a much bigger reward — high risk, high payoff.

pictos.name: Confident Fighter
pictos.effect: 30% increased damage but can't be Healed — leans into the aggression.

characters.statsFocus: Critical Rate (Luck) and Speed; he wants to act fast and crit to climb Ranks.
playstyle: Start fights and immediately work up to S-Rank for the damage multiplier.
Parry and counter relentlessly — Augmented Counter is most of his damage.
Don't let your Rank drop; avoid taking hits that knock you down a tier.

characters.name: Sciel
characters.summary: A slow build to an enormous payoff. Sciel stacks Foretell (Sun/Moon charges) and Dark damage, then detonates for huge hits while scaling her own stats through criticals and survivability.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Big damage bonus while undamaged — pairs with her survivability.

pictos.name: Glass Cannon
pictos.effect: 30%+ damage, no healing — cash in her detonations.

pictos.name: Inverted Affinity
pictos.effect: Turns a drawback into an advantage for her scaling — leans into the all-in.

characters.statsFocus: Luck for Critical Rate plus Defense to survive the build-up; she scales with crits.
playstyle: Stack Foretell charges (Sun/Moon) over a few turns.
Apply Dark and weaken enemies, then unleash her detonation skills for a massive hit.
Use her survivability so the long set-up never gets interrupted.

characters.name: Monoco
characters.summary: The toolbox. Monoco copies enemy abilities through his Bestial Wheel, shreds Break gauges to stagger tough foes, and runs high-Speed parry support to keep the party's AP flowing.

pictos.name: Immaculate
pictos.effect: Damage bonus while undamaged — supported by his parry uptime.

pictos.name: Energising Parry
pictos.effect: Parrying grants AP — fuels the whole party economy.

pictos.name: Empowering Parry
pictos.effect: Parrying grants a damage buff to convert defense into offense.

characters.statsFocus: Speed above all (act first to set up Break), with Defense to keep him on the field.
playstyle: Pick Bestial skills from the Wheel that match the fight (Break, damage or utility).
Focus the enemy Break gauge to stagger them for the whole party.
Parry constantly — Energising Parry refunds AP so the team can spend freely.
cons: Bestial Wheel takes investment to unlock
Reward depends on knowing the fight

characters.name: Gustave
characters.summary: Your starting powerhouse. Gustave charges his arm to unleash Overcharge for big single hits while debuffing and tanking. A strong, beginner-friendly bruiser for the first stretch of the game.

pictos.name: Exposing Attack
pictos.effect: Attacks apply a vulnerability so the party hits harder.

pictos.name: SOS Shell
pictos.effect: Grants protection when low on health — keeps him standing.

pictos.name: Rewarding Mark
pictos.effect: Marking enemies returns resources/AP for more actions.

characters.statsFocus: Vitality for survivability and Agility for turns; build Overcharge then spend it.
playstyle: Charge your arm across turns, then release Overcharge for a huge single hit.
Apply Mark/vulnerability so the team piles on.
Tank for the squad early — he's your sturdy frontliner before the full roster arrives.
cons: Falls off vs. the late-game carries
